a malicious  Word  or  RTF  document  was  sent  to specific  employees In each campaign, a malicious Word or RTF document was sent to specific employees. This is a form of spearphishing where the attacker targets specific individuals with malicious attachments to gain initial access.

This from social isaugmentedwitha engineeringscam personalphonecall theattacker, This technique involves using social engineering over a phone call to persuade the victim to open a malicious email attachment. The attacker's follow-up call to confirm the document was opened adds another layer of social engineering to ensure the malicious payload is executed.

encouraging  the  intended  victim  to  open  the email  attachment  and  click  inside  it This technique involves tricking the user into executing a file that contains malicious code.

screen-shot grabbing  malware Taking screenshots of the victim's desktop to capture sensitive information displayed on the screen.

Persistence  is established  by  creating  a  scheduled  task  that runs the main malware file every 25 minutes Using scheduled tasks to execute programs or scripts at specific times or intervals.

the  INI  file,  using  it  to  issue  commands  to  the compromised machine as well as to reflect the status of previous commands Using command-line interfaces to execute commands.

the  malware  uses "http  POST"  to  send  data  that  has  been retrieved/stolen  from  the  infected  system  to the CnC server The adversary uses an existing command and control channel to exfiltrate data.

Information  is  sent  using  randomized  variable names  and  simple  encryption  on  the  textbaseddatausingbase64encodingand substitution encryption with these alphabets The adversary encrypts files and data to make them inaccessible to users and applications and potentially to exfiltrate information from the victim's network.

uses  SMB  commands (including  TreeConnect  and  Open/Write  AndX) to  locate  a  vulnerable  host Adversaries use network service scanning to locate systems and services that are accessible over the network.

to folderand The  attacking  system  reads  the  log  file  and uses  its  contents  to  then  transfer  a  32-bit  or thevictim's 64-bitexecutable file it. run C:\Windows\Temp Adversaries may transfer tools or files to the victim's system to perform further actions or operations. This technique involves moving executables or other files to the target system.

ThemeterpreterDLL ismade operational  via  reflective  DLL  injection,  which copies  the  meterpreter  DLL  into  the  memory space  of  a  chosen  victim  process  (such  as explorer.exe) and runs it as a thread. dversaries inject code into the address space of another process to execute their payload. Reflective DLL injection is a method of process injection where a DLL is loaded into the address space of a process without the use of traditional Windows APIs.

ThemeterpreterDLL ismade operational  via  reflective  DLL  injection,  which copies  the  meterpreter  DLL  into  the  memory space  of  a  chosen  victim  process  (such  as explorer.exe) and runs it as a thread. Transferring tools or files to the victim's system. This encompasses the initial transfer of the Meterpreter DLL.

The  {~random}  file  names  are  not  really random,  but  instead  a  base64  combination  of the  victim's  system  volume  serial  number  and MAC  address,  which  are  obtained  by  queries to  the  system's  WMI  Service Adversaries use Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) to obtain information.

A PowerShell script created by the infected email attachment This technique involves using PowerShell to execute commands and scripts. The described behavior of using a PowerShell script to handle a base64-encoded payload is a common use case of PowerShell for malicious activities.

contains a base64-encoded block of text that is decoded, This technique includes various methods of obfuscating files or data to avoid detection. Base64 encoding is a common method of obfuscation used to hide the contents of a payload.

written into memory as a thread, and executed. This technique involves injecting code into the memory space of another process. The action of writing the decoded payload into memory and executing it as a thread aligns with process injection techniques.

PowerShell script file created from base64 encoded string PowerShell script file created from a base64 encoded string hidden in a Word document
